title: Update mesh merge to return lane to idle instead of removing worktree
description: Change mesh merge to return the lane worktree to idle state after merge, instead of running git worktree remove. After the PR is merged: checkout wt/{workspace_id} in the lane worktree, reset --hard to origin/main, delete the local task branch, and record the lane as idle in coordination state. The worktree directory survives and is immediately available for the next task. Removes the git worktree remove and branch-delete-before-worktree-remove sequencing that caused MESH-15.
acceptance_criteria: After mesh merge, the lane worktree is checked out on wt/{workspace_id} and synced to origin/main
The task branch is deleted locally and remotely after merge
The worktree directory is NOT removed — it persists for the next task
Lane status in coordination state transitions from active to idle
mesh lane list shows the lane as idle immediately after merge
mesh merge --discard-uncommitted still works (discards changes, then returns to idle)
Tests cover: lane returns to idle, worktree survives, branch deleted, immediate re-claim works
